programmes.show_description: Your Front Row Seat to Justice. Taking you beyond the headlines and into the courtroom, with in-depth legal reporting and expert analysis of the most important and compelling trials.

FL v. AILEEN WUORNOS

FL v. AILEEN WUORNOS
FL v. AILEEN WUORNOS
A legendary high school football coach is killed by a former player.  Was the killer insane or did he know what he was doing?  This small community, rocked by the loss of a leader, wants answers.
When a mother is found dead by her son on their farm, her family members start pointing fingers at each other. But with only so much evidence, will the real killer be found?
A feud between two farmers leaves one man dead and small community in shock. How a dispute over land leads to murder, and the defendant claiming self-defense.
...With Vinnie Politan. The series looks beyond the usual suspects to bring you the gripping stories of everyday people caught up in a world of deception and manipulation as they become accomplices.

Every weekday night on Court TV, Vinnie Politan takes a deep dive into the True Crime stories that are captivating the nation. Now, you can catch the sharp analysis and insightful interviews.
A wife is accused of murdering her husband, whose corpse had been hidden in a closet for over a year. Witness this incredible investigation, trial and the surprising sentence that followed.

Actress Lana Clarkson was found dead in the home of famed music producer Phil Spector. Spector claims she took her own life, while the evidence says otherwise, but what will the jury decide?
What was the motive behind Erik and Lyle Menendez brutally murdering their parents? The prosecution said it was greed, but the defense claimed a dark family secret led to the killings.
Michael Jackson's personal doctor Conrad Murray took an oath to first do no harm. But that's exactly what prosecutors say Murray did when Jackson died from an overdose of a powerful anesthesia drug.
...With Vinnie Politan. The series looks beyond the usual suspects to bring you the gripping stories of everyday people caught up in a world of deception and manipulation as they become accomplices.

Sitcom star Rebecca Schaeffer's career was on the rise, until she opened the door of her Hollywood apartment to face to face with her killer, Robert Bardo.
